data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Design elements are strategically positioned to enhance usability.
data.technical_spec: Interactive elements are adequately sized and placed for ease of access.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Reduces choice overload for users by simplifying decision-making processes.
data.technical_spec: Streamlined navigation and limited action buttons per screen.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Graphs and lists are broken into digestible chunks.
data.technical_spec: Limits information clusters on pages to 7±2 items.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Familiar UI patterns are used to leverage user expectations.
data.technical_spec: Consistent use of standard icons and navigation paths.

data.title: Aesthetic Usability Effect
data.description: Visual design enhances the overall usability perception.
data.technical_spec: Use of a cohesive color palette and typography for aesthetic consistency.
